# Academic Evaluation Requirement

## Overview
This page outlines the academic evaluation requirements for international applicants to BYU-Idaho. It includes accepted evaluation agencies, documentation rules based on earned credits, timing recommendations, policies for students with extensive U.S. college credit, and transfer equivalency processes. Students should review this information before submitting transcripts or ordering an evaluation.

---

## Essential Information

**Accepted Academic Evaluators**  
BYU-Idaho accepts transcript evaluations from **International Education Research Foundation (IERF)** and **TEC (formerly SpanTran)**. These services operate under a joint agreement with BYU, BYU-Hawaii, and Ensign College. References on agency websites may list BYU generally, but the evaluations apply to BYU-Idaho.

Choosing an alternate evaluator may result in added fees and processing delays. Applicants should submit documents to IERF or TEC **4–6 weeks before the admissions deadline** to ensure timely delivery to CES.

**Documentation Requirements by Credit Level**  
- Applicants with **fewer than 24 college semester credits** must submit both **secondary education records** and any **college coursework completed outside the U.S.**  
- Applicants with **24 or more credits completed outside the U.S.** must submit only their **international college coursework** for evaluation.

**IERF**  
IERF is the preferred CES evaluation agency. Applicants should select the **Detail Report** option when ordering an evaluation.

**TEC**  
TEC provides a CES-specific evaluation portal. Applicants should follow the instructions to request a **course analysis** for non-U.S. transcripts.

**International Students With 90+ U.S. Credits**  
International students with **90 or more credits from a U.S. college or university** are **not eligible** to apply for on-campus study. This includes credits earned through BYU-Idaho Online, Pathway, and Institute. These students may continue their studies through BYU-Pathway Worldwide programs.

International students who previously studied on campus at BYU-Idaho and have since exceeded 90 U.S. credits are **eligible to reapply.**

**Transfer Equivalency After Admission**  
Once admitted, university credits must undergo transfer equivalency review. Students should consult BYU-Idaho's international transfer credit site for details.

Source Link: https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply/international-students/finances

---

## Deadlines, Requirements, & Criteria

- **Deadline 1:** Not applicable  
- **Deadline 2:** Not applicable  
- **Requirement / Rule:** Applicants must use IERF or TEC for academic evaluations and must submit required documents based on their completed credits. Students with 90+ U.S. credits may not apply for on-campus study.  

---

## Important Links

- **IERF Evaluation Portal:** https://ierf.org/uni-evaluation/byu-ces/  
- **TEC CES Portal:** https://spanside.my.salesforce-sites.com/SpantranApplication?Id=a743884c-b6cf-42e2-8c0c-3de0dc350c77  
- **International Transfer Credits Site:** https://www.byui.edu/transfer-evaluation/my-credit-transfer#international  
- **Admissions Application:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply  
- **Application Help:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/application-help  

---

# Application Steps

## Overview
This page explains the application process for international students seeking to study on campus at BYU-Idaho. It includes eligibility rules based on completed U.S. college credits, required application steps, visa procedures, SEVIS fee details, and check-in expectations. Students should begin preparing well in advance of the application deadline.

---

## Essential Information

**Eligibility: International Students With 90+ U.S. Credits**  
International students with **90 or more credits earned at any U.S. college or university** are **not eligible** to apply for on-campus study. This includes credits earned through **BYU-Idaho Online, Pathway, and Institute** programs. Students in this situation are encouraged to continue their education through **BYU-Pathway Worldwide**.  
International students who previously studied **on campus at BYU-Idaho** and have since reached 90+ credits **may reapply**.

---

**Application Steps (Start 6 Months Before the Deadline)**

1. **Begin Application**  
   Start your online application using the Apply Now link.  
   Submit the application before the following deadlines:  
   - **Fall:** May 1  
   - **Winter:** September 15  
   - **Spring:** January 15

2. **Deposit**  
   Pay the required international student deposit. Details are available on the **Finances** page.

3. **Mail I-20 Document**  
   BYU-Idaho will mail your Form I-20, which is required to apply for an F-1 visa and to enter the United States.

4. **Pay SEVIS I-901 Fee**  
   Pay the SEVIS fee **at least 3 days** before your visa appointment or, for Canadians, before arriving at the U.S. border.  
   - F-1 Visa: **$350**  
   - J-1 Visa: **$220**

5. **Go to U.S. Embassy for F-1 Visa**  
   Schedule a visa interview at your nearest U.S. embassy or consulate.  
   Bring your **I-901 payment receipt**.  
   *Note: Canadian students do not need to apply for an F-1 visa; it is waived at the border.*

6. **Arrive at School & Check In**  
   You may enter the United States **up to 30 days before** the program start date listed on your I-20.  
   Check in with **International Services** within **two weeks** of the semester start date.

---

## Deadlines, Requirements, & Criteria

- **Deadline 1:** Fall – May 1  
- **Deadline 2:** Winter – September 15  
- **Requirement / Rule:** Students must follow all application steps, pay required fees, and remain eligible for on-campus study (including the 90-credit rule).

---

## Important Links

- **Apply Now:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply  
- **Application Help:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/application-help  
- **Request More Information:** https://byui-5486.page451.sites.451.io  
- **Finances Information:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply/international-students/finances  
- **SEVIS I-901 Fee Payment:** https://www.fmjfee.com/i901fee/index.htm  

---

source: https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply/international-students/proficiency

# English Proficiency Test Requirement

## Overview
This page explains the English proficiency standards required for international applicants to BYU-Idaho, including minimum test scores, accepted testing formats, guidance for improving English skills, and circumstances in which a waiver may be considered. Students should review this information before submitting their admissions application.

---

## Essential Information
Required Scores on Proficiency Tests  
Prospective students taking an English proficiency test must meet the minimum required score for the individual subject tests AND the overall composite score for the exam. If English is your second language, you must meet the following English proficiency requirements (TOEFL, IELTS, PTE, or Cambridge English).

### Required Minimum Scores

| Subject Area / Test | TOEFL | IELTS | PTE | Cambridge English |
|---------------------|-------|-------|-----|-------------------|
| Reading             | 17    | 6.0   | 52  | 169               |
| Writing             | 17    | 5.0   | 52  | 154               |
| Listening           | 16    | 6.0   | 52  | 169               |
| Speaking            | 16    | 5.5   | 52  | 162               |
| Use of English      | Not Applicable | Not Applicable | Not Applicable | 169 |
| Composite           | 79    | 6.5   | 52  | 169               |

**TOEFL**  
The TOEFL iBT is the Test of English as a Foreign Language Internet-based test. The TOEFL exam is administered by the Educational Testing Service numerous times each year throughout the world. BYU-Idaho only accepts the TOEFL iBT, TOEFL iBT Home Edition, and TOEFL iBT Paper Edition. Scores from the TOEFL Essentials, TOEFL PBT (paper-based test), and other versions will not be considered.

**IELTS**  
The IELTS is the International English Language Testing System and is administered numerous times each year throughout the world. For more information about testing times and locations, visit the IELTS page.

**PTE**  
The PTE is the Pearson Test of English. Applicants who choose this exam must take the PTE Academic Exam, not the general Pearson Test of English. You may obtain more information on the Pearson page.

**Cambridge English Qualifications**  
Cambridge English Tests are produced by the IELTS company. BYU-Idaho will only accept scores from the C1 Advanced, C2 Proficiency, and B2 First exams.

**English Language Help**  
BYU-Idaho does not offer an ESL (English as a Second Language) program. Students who do not meet the required proficiency scores are encouraged to continue developing their English skills and retest. The English Language Center at Brigham Young University may also be able to help you improve your skills. Learn more at <http://elc.byu.edu>.

**English Proficiency Waiver Requests**  
BYU-Idaho rarely grants English proficiency waivers. These waivers are intended for applicants who have demonstrated readiness for academic work in English. Only applicants who meet one of the following criteria will be reviewed:

1. Successful completion of 24 or more credits in the past two years on campus at an accredited U.S. college or university with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0. (PathwayConnect credit will not count toward the 24 credits.)
2. Completion of all years of high school in the U.S.

Waiver requests can be submitted to `admprocessing@byui.edu`. Official transcripts verifying college or high school academics must be sent to `transcriptsubmit@byui.edu` and processed before the waiver request is reviewed. Waiver requests are reviewed by a committee, and approval is not guaranteed.

---

## Deadlines, Requirements, & Criteria

- **Deadline 1:** Not applicable  
- **Deadline 2:** Not applicable  
- **Requirement / Rule:** Students must meet the minimum English proficiency scores or qualify for an approved waiver.  

---

## Important Links

- **TOEFL Information:** https://www.ets.org/toefl  
- **IELTS Information:** https://www.ielts.org  
- **PTE Academic Information:** https://www.pearsonpte.com  
- **Cambridge English Exams:** https://www.cambridgeenglish.org  
- **BYU English Language Center:** http://elc.byu.edu  
- **Admissions Application:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply  

---

# International Student Finances

## Overview
This page explains the financial expectations for international students attending BYU-Idaho, including the I-20 requirement, proof of financial support, cost of attendance, dependent costs, deposits, and sponsorship rules. Students should review this information before applying for an I-20 or submitting financial documentation to the University.

---

## Essential Information

**Get an I-20**  
Before you come to BYU-Idaho as an F-1 international student, you'll need to get an I-20. You can obtain one from the International Services Office. An I-20 allows you to become eligible to apply for a student visa and come to the U.S. to study.

**Proof of Financial Support**  
U.S. immigration law requires you to include proof of financial support when applying to BYU-Idaho. You must show that you have enough funds to support yourself during your college experience. Proof can come from you or a sponsor.

**Cost of Attending BYU-Idaho**  
BYU-Idaho's International Services office reports to U.S. Immigration the average annual cost of attendance for international students. This amount is:

- $22,250 annual for members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints  
- $27,000 annual for non-members of the Church  

The total amount you will need for all four years at BYU-Idaho is:

- $89,000 for members of the Church  
- $108,000 for non-members of the Church  

Once you arrive at BYU-Idaho, if you or your sponsor do not have enough funds to cover your expenses, you will be asked to return home and complete your degree online.

**Cost for Dependents**  
Any dependents who come with you will need:

- An F-2 visa  
- An additional $9,000 in available funding for the first dependent  
- $4,500 for each additional dependent  

**Basic Educational Financial Plan**  
The tables below summarize the I-20 financial plan.

### Cost of Attendance

| Type of Expense           | Member | Non-Member |
|---------------------------|--------|------------|
| Tuition and Fees          | $4,800 | $8,600     |
| Health Insurance          | $1,608 | $1,608     |
| Personal Living Expenses  | $9,066 | $9,066     |
| Housing/Food              | $7,812 | $7,812     |
| Books                     | $572   | $572       |

### Additional Expenses

| Additional Expense        | Member        | Non-Member    |
|---------------------------|---------------|---------------|
| IERF Evaluation           | $180-$200     | $180-$200     |
| English Proficiency Test  | $150-$250     | $150-$250     |
| Immigration Fees          | SEVIS $220-$350 | SEVIS $220-$350 |
| Foreign Deposit           | $4,000        | $6,000        |
| Visa Application          | $160          | $160          |

**Scholarships and Financial Aid**  
There are no scholarships exclusively for international students. BYU-Idaho scholarships are available to all students who meet the qualifications. Visit Financial Aid to learn more about what is available and when to apply.

**Deposit Policy**  
International students (except Canadian internationals) must make a deposit of at least $4,000 before the I-20 can be created. The deposit is refundable if you decide not to attend.

**Sponsorship: Who can become a sponsor?**  
Anyone who is willing and financially able to assist you can be your sponsor. This may include a friend, parent, sibling, etc. It is your responsibility to find a sponsor.

Source Link: https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply/international-students/finances

---

## Deadlines, Requirements, & Criteria

- **Deadline 1:** Not applicable  
- **Deadline 2:** Not applicable  
- **Requirement / Rule:** Students must demonstrate sufficient funding to receive an I-20 and remain eligible for on-campus study.  

---

## Important Links

- **I-20 Application Process:** https://www.byui.edu/international-services/i-20-application-process  
- **Dependent Rules:** https://www.irs.gov/credits-deductions/individuals/dependents  
- **BYU-Idaho Scholarships:** https://www.byui.edu/financial-aid/?cid=fli:122  
- **Admissions Application:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/apply  
- **Application Help:** https://www.byui.edu/admissions/application-help  
- **Request More Information:** https://byui-5486.page451.sites.451.io  

---
